ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When the sky has split [open]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the sky bursts open,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has responded to its Lord and was obligated [to do so]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: obeying its Lord as it must,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the earth has been extended
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and when the earth is flattened out,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has cast out that within it and relinquished [it]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ejects ˹all˺ its contents and becomes empty,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And has responded to its Lord and was obligated [to do so] -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: obeying its Lord as it must, ˹surely you will all be judged˺.

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَدۡحٗا فَمُلَٰقِيهِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya ayyuha alinsanu innaka kadihun ila rabbika kadhan famulaqeehi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O mankind, indeed you are laboring toward your Lord with [great] exertion and will meet it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O humanity! Indeed, you are labouring restlessly towards your Lord, and will ˹eventually˺ meet the consequences.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then as for he who is given his record in his right hand,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who are given their record in their right hand,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will be judged with an easy account
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: they will have an easy reckoning,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And return to his people in happiness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and will return to their people joyfully.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّا مَنۡ أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ وَرَآءَ ظَهۡرِهِۦ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma man ootiya kitabahu waraa thahrihi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But as for he who is given his record behind his back,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And as for those who are given their record ˹in their left hand˺ from behind their backs,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will cry out for destruction
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: they will cry for ˹instant˺ destruction,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [enter to] burn in a Blaze.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and will burn in the blazing Fire.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he had [once] been among his people in happiness;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For they used to be prideful among their people,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he had thought he would never return [to Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: thinking they would never return ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلَىٰٓۚ إِنَّ رَبَّهُۥ كَانَ بِهِۦ بَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bala inna rabbahu kana bihi baseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But yes! Indeed, his Lord was ever of him, Seeing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yes ˹they would˺! Surely their Lord has always been All-Seeing of them.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So I swear by the twilight glow
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So, I do swear by the twilight!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the night and what it envelops
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the night and whatever it envelops!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the moon when it becomes full
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And by the moon when it waxes full!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [That] you will surely experience state after state.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will certainly pass from one state to another.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So what is [the matter] with them [that] they do not believe,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So what is the matter with them that they do not believe,

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا قُرِئَ عَلَيۡهِمُ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ لَا يَسۡجُدُونَۤ۩
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha quria AAalayhimu alquranu la yasjudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the Qur'an is recited to them, they do not prostrate [to Allah]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and when the Quran is recited to them, they do not bow down ˹in submission˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But those who have disbelieved deny,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, the disbelievers persist in denial.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Allah is most knowing of what they keep within themselves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But Allah knows best whatever they hide.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So give them tidings of a painful punishment,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So give them good news of a painful punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ لَهُمۡ أَجۡرٌ غَيۡرُ مَمۡنُونِۭ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lahum ajrun ghayru mamnoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them is a reward uninterrupted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But those who believe and do good will have a never-ending reward.
